policy: Thresholds are decision-support gates, not universal medicinal chemistry laws. Each value carries provenance so it can be replaced by target-specific calibration.

activity_pchembl_lower_bound_min.label: Conservative EGFR activity floor
activity_pchembl_lower_bound_min.source: ChEMBL pChEMBL convention; operational early-lead triage policy
activity_pchembl_lower_bound_min.rationale: pChEMBL 7 corresponds to 100 nM when derived from IC50/Ki/Kd in molar units. Target-SAFE uses the lower prediction bound, not the mean, to avoid overclaiming.

applicability_similarity_min.label: Minimum nearest-analog similarity
applicability_similarity_min.source: Target-SAFE applicability-domain calibration on the EGFR reference set
applicability_similarity_min.rationale: Below this level, the candidate is treated as outside the analog evidence domain. Live ChEMBL training can replace this static floor with a scaffold-split calibration.

evidence_confidence_min_for_go.label: Minimum graph evidence support for Go
evidence_confidence_min_for_go.rationale: Go requires at least moderate tool-grounded support. Lower values keep the candidate in Hold until assay, analog, or external evidence is added.
evidence_confidence_min_for_go.computed_from: analog support + data availability + descriptor reliability + alert penalties

logp_extreme_max.label: Extreme logP cap
logp_extreme_max.source: Lipinski rule-of-five context with a relaxed early-lead triage margin
logp_extreme_max.rationale: High lipophilicity can drive ADMET risk; this cap prevents confident Go calls.
logp_extreme_max.computed_from: RDKit Crippen MolLogP or deterministic fallback descriptor model

molecular_weight_extreme_max.label: Extreme molecular weight cap
molecular_weight_extreme_max.source: Lipinski rule-of-five context with a relaxed kinase-inhibitor triage margin
molecular_weight_extreme_max.rationale: Used only as an extreme hard gate; softer MW concerns remain in Hold uncertainty.
molecular_weight_extreme_max.computed_from: RDKit MolWt or deterministic fallback descriptor model

prediction_interval_width_max.label: Maximum acceptable activity uncertainty
prediction_interval_width_max.rationale: A broad interval means the model is not precise enough for Go. The candidate can still remain Hold when the mean looks promising.
prediction_interval_width_max.computed_from: upper prediction bound - lower prediction bound

qed_min_floor.label: Very low QED floor
qed_min_floor.source: RDKit QED implementation of Bickerton et al. Quantifying the chemical beauty of drugs
qed_min_floor.rationale: A very low QED is treated as a hard early-triage warning rather than a potency claim.
qed_min_floor.computed_from: RDKit QED or deterministic fallback descriptor model

sa_score_max.label: Synthetic accessibility risk cap
sa_score_max.source: Ertl-Schuffenhauer synthetic accessibility score interpretation; Target-SAFE fallback policy
sa_score_max.rationale: High SA candidates should not be advanced without synthetic route review.
sa_score_max.computed_from: RDKit-backed or deterministic fallback SA approximation

tpsa_extreme_max.label: Extreme TPSA cap
tpsa_extreme_max.source: Veber oral bioavailability context with relaxed early-lead triage margin
tpsa_extreme_max.rationale: Used as a conservative hard gate for very polar candidates in this oral TKI pilot.
tpsa_extreme_max.computed_from: RDKit TPSA or deterministic fallback descriptor model
